mnemonics: The world state is like a bank's live account ledger at this very moment. It doesn't show every single transaction ever made, but it does show everyone's exact, current balance.
keyPoints: The world state is a database that holds the current state of all accounts (balances, contract code, storage).
It is a mapping of address to account state, often implemented as a key-value store.
It is separate from the blockchain itself, which is a historical record of transactions. The world state is derived by executing all transactions in sequence.
commonMistakes: Thinking the world state is stored directly in the blocks. It is stored separately and updated with each new block.
Confusing the world state (current data) with the transaction history (historical data).
Assuming all blockchains have an identical world state structure. The exact implementation (e.g., Patricia Merkle Tries in Ethereum) can vary.
